pi_items.source: Verified in task.json (line 17), setup_task.sh (line 31), and export_result.sh (line 14). Path is consistently used across all task scripts.

Verified in task.json (line 19) and export_result.sh (line 16). Follows standard naming convention for SHA-256 checksum files.
Verified in task.json (line 20) and export_result.sh (line 17). Standard naming for manifest documentation.

Verified in task.json (line 21) and task description. Both device types confirmed as legitimate OpenICE device types via (1) web search of OpenICE documentation at openice.info, (2) GitHub repository https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp, (3) evidence files in evidence_docs/ directory confirming these device types are available in OpenICE Supervisor. Web search confirms OpenICE supports both multiparameter monitors and infusion pumps as integrated device types.

Install script (install_openice.sh line 66) clones from https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp using --depth 1 (latest version). Environment uses Java 17 and Gradle build system. Requires OpenICE 2.0 or later (per install_openice.sh line 12).

Verified across multiple task files (create_simulated_device/export_result.sh, infusion_safety_interlock/setup_task.sh, icu_monitoring_setup/export_result.sh, alarm_audit/export_result.sh). Also mentioned in clinical_app_tour/README.md. Task description states to check '/home/ga/openice/logs/' for the active OpenICE log file. OpenICE 1.3.0+ stores logs in user home directory per https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp/releases/tag/v1.3.0.

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE is a real, open-source medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital (https://github.com/mdpnp/mdpnp). The task requires creating an audit trail preservation workflow with TWO specific device types: Multiparameter Monitor and Infusion Pump, which are verified as legitimate device types in OpenICE. All metadata filenames and directory paths are accurately specified and consistently used across setup, export, and verification scripts. The data generated is SYNTHETIC, produced by OpenICE's simulated device adapters, not from real patient data. The audit directory path is /home/ga/Desktop/QA_Audit_2026, containing three required files: device_session_audit.log (copied from /home/ga/openice/logs/openice.log), integrity_hash.sha256 (SHA-256 checksum), and audit_manifest.txt (metadata listing).
